risk_if_not_implemented: Without Contacts With Groups & Associations, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Threat Intelligence Program
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to implement a threat intelligence program that includes a cross-organization information-sharing capability that can influence the development of the system and security architectures, selection of security solutions, monitoring, threat hunting, response and recovery activities.
compensating_control_1.justification: Threat Intelligence Program (THR-01) provides policy-level governance that compensates for the absence of Contacts With Groups & Associations (GOV-07) by establishing documented expectations, accountability structures, and organizational guardrails. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Security, Compliance & Resilience Assessments
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to regularly review processes and documented procedures to ensure conformity with the organization's security, compliance and/or resilience policies, standards and other applicable requirements.
compensating_control_2.justification: Security, Compliance & Resilience Assessments (CPL-03) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of Contacts With Groups & Associations (GOV-07) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
